# nmcli monitor — sample event lines (T8 spike fixture)
#
# Reconstructed shape of `nmcli monitor` stdout on NetworkManager 1.x (Debian
# bookworm / Armbian target). nmcli/mmcli are NOT installed on the current dev
# machine, so these lines are based on the NetworkManager reference manual
# (networkmanager.dev/docs/api/latest/nmcli.html) and published real-world
# captures (oneuptime.com nmcli-monitor guide, linuxcommandlibrary), kept
# faithful to the actual emitted format.
#
# `nmcli monitor` (the GLOBAL monitor) emits THREE line shapes:
#   1. Device line:      "<ifname>: <state>"  or  "<ifname>: <state> to \"<conn>\""
#   2. Connection line:  "\"<conn-name>\" (<type>, <ip/cidr>): connection <verb>"
#   3. Global line:      "NetworkManager is now in the '<state>' state"
#                        "Connectivity is now '<state>'"
#
# IMPORTANT: `nmcli monitor` is HUMAN-READABLE. It has NO --terse / -t mode and
# NO stable field separator. Parsing keys on the leading token before the first
# ": " for device lines (that token is the ifname — the chosen event key, see
# MONITOR-NOTES.md "Event keying decision"). Connection lines start with a
# quote (") and are de-prioritised by the parser.
#
# Lines starting with '#' are fixture comments, NOT emitted by nmcli.
# Device states (per `nmcli device monitor`): connecting | connected |
# disconnected | unavailable | unmanaged | deactivating.

# ---- Ethernet cable plugged in, DHCP completes ----
eth0: connecting (prepare)
eth0: connecting (getting IP configuration)
eth0: connected to "Wired connection 1"
"Wired connection 1" (ethernet, 192.168.1.100/24): connection activated

# ---- Ethernet cable unplugged ----
eth0: disconnected
"Wired connection 1" (ethernet): connection deactivated
eth0: unavailable

# ---- WiFi: associate + authenticate + DHCP to an AP ----
wlan0: connecting (prepare)
wlan0: connecting (need authentication)
wlan0: connecting (getting IP configuration)
wlan0: connected to "FieldHotspot-5G"
"FieldHotspot-5G" (wifi, 10.0.5.42/24): connection activated

# ---- WiFi: drop / roam away from AP ----
wlan0: deactivating
wlan0: disconnected

# ---- USB WiFi adapter HOTPLUG: device appears, becomes managed ----
# (a brand-new ifname shows up mid-stream — this is an ADD event)
wlan1: unavailable
wlan1: disconnected

# ---- USB WiFi adapter UNPLUGGED: device goes unmanaged then vanishes ----
# (no explicit "removed" line; the ifname simply stops appearing — REMOVAL must
#  be inferred by reconciling event-seen devices against a poll/snapshot, see
#  the event-vs-poll table in MONITOR-NOTES.md)
wlan1: unmanaged

# ---- Cellular / WWAN modem brought up by NetworkManager ----
ww0: connecting (prepare)
ww0: connecting (getting IP configuration)
ww0: connected to "gsm-operator"
"gsm-operator" (gsm, 100.64.0.7/30): connection activated

# ---- Cellular modem lost signal / disconnected ----
ww0: disconnected
ww0: unavailable

# ---- Global NetworkManager connectivity transitions ----
Connectivity is now 'limited'
Connectivity is now 'full'
Connectivity is now 'none'

# ---- Global NetworkManager state transitions ----
NetworkManager is now in the 'connected (site only)' state
NetworkManager is now in the 'connected (global)' state
NetworkManager is now in the 'disconnected' state

# ---- NetworkManager RESTART seen by a long-lived `nmcli monitor` ----
# The monitor process does NOT exit on `systemctl restart NetworkManager`; it
# reconnects over D-Bus and emits stopped/running notices, then resumes:
NetworkManager is stopped
NetworkManager is running
NetworkManager is now in the 'connecting' state
NetworkManager is now in the 'connected (global)' state
